summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes XOR-encrypted Windows binary from third-party GitHub account
details: On install, the package's postinstall hook (install-hook.js) executes a base64-encoded UTF-16LE PowerShell command that downloads launcher.bin from https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin (a personal GitHub account unrelated to the package publisher), XORs each byte with 0x42, writes the decrypted result to %TEMP%\tmp.exe, and launches it hidden via Start-Process. After triggering the payload, a second detached hidden PowerShell process sleeps 30 seconds then recursively force-removes the installed package directory to destroy forensic artifacts. The advertised library functionality is a decoy: index.js is a stub that only logs simulated email-confirmation messages and performs no real work. The package's only actual effect on a Windows installer is to fetch and execute attacker-controlled code with the installer's privileges, then erase itself.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
